title: 10 best herbal capsules for dry nose
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for dry nose are natural supplements that help alleviate dry, itchy, and congested nasal passages by soothing and moisturizing the mucous membranes. These capsules typically contain a blend of potent herbs such as Eucalyptus globulus, Echinacea purpurea, Ginkgo biloba, Zingiber officinale, Ephedra sinica, Thymus serpyllum, Rosmarinus officinalis, Thymus vulgaris, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Curcuma longa. If left untreated, dry nose can lead to fatigue, difficulty sleeping, and decreased productivity, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower capsules helps with dry nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated nasal passages. The capsules contain a concentrated extract of echinacea, a herb known for its ability to reduce swelling and congestion in the respiratory tract. By reducing inflammation, purple coneflower capsules can help alleviate discomfort and restore normal mucus production, leaving your nose feeling moist and comfortable again. This natural remedy is an effective way to combat dryness and irritation caused by allergies, colds, or sinus infections.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The capsules contain anti-inflammatory compounds, such as quercetin and kaempferol, which help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages and sinuses, alleviating symptoms of dryness and congestion.

Antioxidant properties: Purple coneflower contains antioxidants like rosmarinic acid, which protect the nasal mucosa from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy mucus production and reducing dryness.

Immunomodulatory effects: The capsules may also exhibit immunomodulatory effects, regulating the immune response in the nasal passages and sinuses to reduce inflammation and promote healing, which can help alleviate symptoms of a dry nose.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the purple coneflower contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to soothe and calm dry, irritated nasal passages.
Seeds: Purple coneflower seeds have been shown to have antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the nasal mucosa.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried echinacea purpurea flowers in sufficient quantity for the desired yield of capsules.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers to remove any dirt or excess moisture using a clean cloth.
Crush the dried echinacea purpurea flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle to facilitate extraction.
Mix one part of the powdered echinacea with two parts of tapioca starch in a bowl for a 1:2 ratio using a wooden spoon.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture and seal them tightly using a capsule filler machine for a total preparation time of 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow capsules helps with dry nose because they contain mucilages, which are natural soothing agents that lubricate and calm the nasal passages. The capsules work by coating the delicate tissues inside the nose, providing a protective barrier against dryness and irritation. This relief is especially beneficial for individuals who experience frequent dry air exposure, allergies, or cold weather conditions. By using marshmallow capsules regularly, you can say goodbye to dry, congested noses and hello to a healthy, comfortable breathing experience.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow root contains mucilages, which are thick, protective coatings that soothe and moisturize the mucous membranes in the nose, helping to alleviate dryness and irritation.
Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in marshmallow capsules help protect the nasal tissues from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate dryness and inflammation in the nose.

Saponins: Saponins are a type of compound found in marshmallow root that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, which helps to soothe and lubricate the nasal passages, reducing dryness and discomfort.
Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of glycosides, which provide anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce swelling and irritation in the nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh marigold flowers at dawn or early morning when they are most potent for best results.
Dry the harvested marigold flowers in a warm and well-ventilated area for 24 hours to preserve potency.
Grind 10 grams of dried marigold flowers into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 5 grams of powdered marigold with 100 milliliters of cold water to create a soothing herbal extract.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the extracted herbal mixture and seal for easy consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with dry nose because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When taken as a supplement, it can help to soothe and moisturize the mucous membranes in the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and irritation caused by dry air or allergies. As a result, herbal licorice capsules can provide effective relief from dry, congested, or scratchy noses, leaving them feeling smoother and more comfortable.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: A compound found in licorice root that has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the nasal passages.

Flavonoids: Licorice contains flavonoids like isoflavones and flavanones, which have antioxidant properties that can help protect the nasal mucosa from damage caused by free radicals and oxidative stress.

Saponins: Licorice root contains saponins, which are natural anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, making it easier to breathe and relieving dryness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to soothe and moisturize the nasal passages, providing relief from dryness and irritation.
Barks: The barks of the licorice plant have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the nasal passages associated with dry nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra for the preparation process.
Grind 30 grams of dried roots into a fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered root with 60 ml of boiling distilled water for 15 minutes.
Filter the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any impurities.
Fill clean capsules with 250 mg of the filtered liquid extract and seal tightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Slippery elm capsules helps with dry nose because of its unique properties that provide relief from dryness, itchiness, and irritation. The mucilages present in slippery elm help to soothe and protect the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy mucus production. This natural remedy also helps to lubricate the nasal cavity, making it easier to breathe and alleviating congestion. As a result, slippery elm capsules provide effective relief from dry nose symptoms, allowing for comfortable breathing and improved overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Slippery elm's mucilages form a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, helping to soothe and moisturize the nasal passages and reduce inflammation caused by dryness.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: The herbal capsule contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, further relieving dryness and discomfort.
Antioxidants: Slippery elm's antioxidants help protect the nasal tissues from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to chronic dryness and other issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that helps soothe and moisturize the nasal passages.
Barks: Contain compounds like mucilages and tannins, which help to calm inflammation and reduce irritation in the nasal cavity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ulmus rubra leaves and stems, drying them immediately to preserve their potency.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for approximately 10 minutes.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powdered ulmus rubra with 500 milligrams of vegetable glycerin in a small bowl for 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture, making sure to leave about 1/8 inch at the top for sealing.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to one year or refrigerate for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme capsules helps with dry nose because of its natural antihistamine properties that combat inflammation and congestion. The herb's antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds help to soothe and calm the nasal passages, reducing redness and irritation. Additionally, thyme's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out excess mucus, allowing for easier breathing and a more comfortable nose. Regular use of herbal thyme capsules can provide long-lasting relief from dry, scratchy noses and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial compounds, such as thymol, help combat fungal and bacterial infections that can contribute to dry nose and sinusitis.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in thyme, including flavonoids and terpenes, reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, alleviating dryness and discomfort.

Expectorant properties: Thyme's natural expectorants, such as thymol and camphor, help loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, relieving congestion and dryness caused by colds, allergies, or sinus infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots are used due to their rich content of phenolic compounds, which help to soothe and moisturize the nasal passages.
Barks: The barks of thyme plants contain flavonoids and terpenoids that exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving dryness and congestion in the nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of dried thymus vulgaris leaves from a reputable supplier or your own garden.
Grind the dried thymus vulgaris leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground thymus vulgaris powder with 100 grams of microcrystalline cellulose in a bowl until well combined.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the thymus vulgaris mixture per capsule and seal tightly.
Store the herbal capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint capsules helps with dry nose because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the nasal passages. The menthol content in peppermint relaxes the nasal tissues, reducing congestion and relieving discomfort associated with a dry nose. Additionally, peppermint's expectorant properties help to loosen and clear out mucus, promoting healthy drainage and restoring normal nasal function. This natural remedy provides quick and effective relief from the discomfort of a dry nose.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as menthol and methyl salicylate, help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, which can alleviate dryness and discomfort.

Mucolytic properties: The mucolytic enzymes present in peppermint capsules break down excess mucus, helping to loosen and clear out dried-out mucus from the nasal passages and sinuses, promoting easier breathing.

Decongestant properties: Peppermint's natural decongestant properties help reduce swelling in the nasal membranes, allowing for easier breathing and relieving congestion caused by dry air or allergies.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in menthol, a natural decongestant that helps to relieve nasal congestion and dryness.
Barks: Containing salicylic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or a trusted supplier weighing approximately 20 grams.
Dry the peppermint leaves using a food dehydrator set at 40 degrees celsius for six hours.
Grind the dried peppermint leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder and sift through a 60-mesh sieve to remove any lumps.
Mix one teaspoon of the ground peppermint powder with 200ml of distilled water creating a gentle infusion that steeps for ten minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the infused peppermint liquid and seal tightly storing them in an airtight container for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder capsules helps with dry nose because they are rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and essential oils that have natural anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds work together to soothe and moisturize the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and congestion caused by dryness. As a result, elder capsules can help alleviate symptoms of dry nose, such as stuffiness, irritation, and discomfort. Regular use of elder capsules may also strengthen the immune system and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Elderberry's antioxidants help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nasal passages, which can contribute to a dry and irritated nose.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Elderberry's anti-inflammatory compounds may help to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with a dry, congested nose.
Mucilage content: The mucilage in elderberry capsules forms a soothing film on the nasal membranes, providing relief from dryness and irritation by locking in moisture.
remedy_parts: Roots: Elder roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm dry, irritated nasal passages.
Barks: Elder barks contain compounds that work as natural decongestants, helping to reduce nasal congestion and relieve a dry, stuffy nose.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in the morning dew.
Dry the harvested sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in a warm dark place for 7 days.
Blend one part dried sambucus nigra with three parts dried marshmallow root and store mixture in an airtight container for up to six months.
Grind the blended mixture into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for at least 10 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with 400mg of the powdered herbal blend and seal with a cotton plug.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger capsules helps with dry nose because of their natural anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and moisturize the nasal passages. The capsaicin present in ginger also stimulates the production of mucous, helping to keep the nose hydrated and comfortable. Additionally, ginger has been shown to improve blood circulation, which may help to increase oxygenation and reduce dryness in the nasal tissues. Overall, taking herbal ginger capsules can provide fast and effective relief from dry, stuffy noses.
remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, relieving dryness and discomfort.
Mucus thinning and clearing: The natural mucolytic properties of ginger break down thick mucus, making it easier to expel and reducing congestion caused by a dry nose.

Antioxidant and antimicrobial activity: Ginger's antioxidant and antimicrobial compounds help protect the nasal passages from oxidative stress and infection, promoting overall respiratory health and potentially helping to reduce dryness and irritation.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of ginger are rich in compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to alleviate dry nose symptoms.
Roots: Ginger roots contain volatile oils that help to reduce nasal congestion and inflammation, making them a popular choice for medicinal capsules for dry nose.
remedy_recipe: Grind dried ginger root into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 30 minutes.
Sift the powdered ginger through a fine-mesh sieve to remove any lumps or large particles.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with one-quarter teaspoon of the sifted ginger powder per capsule.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.
Dispense the herbal capsules as directed by a qualified healthcare professional or as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric capsules helps with dry nose because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce nasal congestion and dryness. The curcumin present in turmeric has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, thereby alleviating symptoms of a dry nose such as itchiness, irritation, and difficulty breathing. Additionally, turmeric's natural antihistamine effects can also help to soothe and calm irritated nasal passages, providing long-lasting relief from dry nose discomfort.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: Reduces inflammation and oxidative stress in the nasal passages, helping to alleviate dryness and discomfort by promoting a healthy mucous membrane environment.
Corticosteroid-like compounds: Help to reduce swelling and congestion in the nasal passages, which can contribute to dryness and discomfort by improving airflow and sinus drainage.
Antioxidant properties: Protects the nasal tissues from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate dryness and irritation, and helps to promote a healthy environment for mucous production.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Containing a high concentration of curcumin, the rhyzome of turmeric has anti-inflammatory properties that help to alleviate symptoms of dry nose.
Roots: The roots of turmeric have been traditionally used to treat respiratory issues, including dry nose, due to their expectorant and antiseptic properties.
remedy_recipe: Weigh and prepare 500 grams of dried turmeric root powder curcuma longa.
Mix the turmeric powder with 200 grams of gelatin powder in a bowl.
Add 50 milliliters of distilled water and 25 milliliters of glycerin to the mixture stirring until it forms a smooth paste.
Fill capsule shells with the powder mixture using a powder filling machine or manually spooning it into the caps.
Seal the filled capsules by applying gentle heat from a heat gun or let them dry at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with dry nose because they contain flavonoids, carotenoids, and saponins that work synergistically to provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant benefits. These active compounds help to soothe and moisturize the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with a dry nose. Additionally, pot marigold's antiseptic properties also help to combat any underlying infections or irritants that may be contributing to the dryness. As a result, herbal pot marigold capsules can provide relief from dry nose symptoms, promoting healthier and more comfortable nasal function.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Calendula's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce swelling and irritation in the nasal passages, which can alleviate symptoms of a dry nose.

Antioxidant property: The antioxidants present in Pot Marigold capsules neutralize free radicals that can damage nasal tissues, promoting healthy mucus production and reducing the risk of chronic dryness.
Softenening and protective property: Calendula's mucilages (gums) help soothe and lubricate the nasal membranes, protecting them from further irritation and desiccation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, pot marigold roots help soothe and calm dry nasal passages.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain fatty acids and omega-3 essential oils, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healthy mucous production in the nasal passages.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula flowers and dry them using a food dehydrator for 2 hours at 95°f.
Combine 200g of dried calendula flowers with 400ml of boiling water in a large ceramic bowl.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes to allow the calendula essence to infuse into the water.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve and discard the solids, reserving the infused liquid.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with 500mg of the calendula-infused liquid and store in an airtight container for up to 6 months.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat dry nose with herbal capsules is by taking a supplement that contains herbs like Ginkgo biloba, which helps to moisturize and soothe the nasal passages. Another effective option is Echinacea, which has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce nasal congestion and irritation. Additionally, Slippery Elm helps to protect and heal the mucous membranes in the nose, restoring its natural moisture barrier.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to dry nose that are treated with herbal capsules are conditions such as bronchitis, sinusitis, and asthma. Herbal capsules containing herbs like Echinacea, Marshmallow root, and Goldenseal help soothe and moisturize the respiratory tract, relieving congestion and inflammation. They also have anti-inflammatory properties that ease coughing and wheezing associated with these conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus capsules helps with dry nose because they contain the active ingredient eucalyptol, a natural decongestant that helps to thin and clear mucus from the nasal passages. This can provide quick relief from congestion and dryness, allowing the nose to breathe more easily. By reducing inflammation and promoting drainage, Eucalyptus globulus capsules can help to alleviate dry nose symptoms, promoting a healthier and more comfortable respiratory system. It also has a soothing effect on the nasal tissue.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene helps to thin and loosen mucus, relieving congestion and providing temporary relief from a dry, stuffy nose.
Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce nasal swelling and alleviate discomfort associated with a dry nose.
Piperitone: This phenolic compound has expectorant properties, helping to break down and clear out mucus, making it easier to breathe through a dry, congested nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Eucalyptus globulus leaves are commonly used to make capsules for dry nose due to their high eucalyptol content, which provides decongestant and expectorant properties.
Barks: Eucalyptus globulus barks are often used in dry nose capsules because of their ability to moisturize and soothe the nasal passages, reducing inflammation and congestion.
Buds: Eucalyptus globulus buds are sometimes used in dry nose capsules as they contain essential oils that help to clear mucus and reduce nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves and clean them with a soft brush.
Dry the leaves in a warm air oven at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind 5 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powder with 500 milligrams of gelatin in a small bowl.
Fill 1000-capsule gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the eucalyptus globulus powder mixture and seal immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea capsules helps with dry nose because they are rich in anti-inflammatory properties that combat irritation and swelling in the nasal passages. The herbal extract stimulates the production of mucus, which helps to moisturize and soothe the dry, cracked tissues. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea has antiviral and antimicrobial properties that protect the nasal area from infections, promoting a healthy and balanced environment for the nasal mucosa to function properly, leading to a reduction in dryness and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Cichoric Acid: Helps to reduce inflammation in the nasal passages, relieving dryness and discomfort.
Caffeic Acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to soothing and moisturizing the nasal mucosa.
Isoquinoline Alkaloids: Stimulates the production of mucus, helping to keep the nasal passages moist and reducing the sensation of dryness.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They contain high levels of alkylamides and phenolic acids, which are believed to help soothe and moisturize the nasal passages.
Roots: The roots are rich in inulin and other polysaccharides, which are thought to stimulate the immune system and aid in reducing inflammation in the nasal passages.
Leaves: The leaves contain compounds like caffeic acid and chicoric acid, which have anti-inflammatory properties and may help to reduce nasal congestion and dryness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the echinacea purpurea roots and flowers at the right time when they are fully ripe and dry.
Dry the plant material in a single layer at 30 degrees celsius for 24 hours to prevent spoilage.
Grind the dried echinacea purpurea roots and flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder at 2 ounces.
Mix the powder with a binding agent like cellulose at a 10 to 1 ratio to form a uniform blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the herbal blend using a capsule filling machine and 500 mg capsules.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba capsules helps with dry nose because of its ability to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the nasal passages. The antioxidants present in Ginkgo biloba can help to protect the nasal mucosa from damage and promote healing. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce congestion and promote the production of mucus, making it easier to breathe and reducing the discomfort associated with a dry nose. Regular use may also help to reduce sinus pressure and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with dry nose by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to the nasal mucosa, which can alleviate dryness and congestion.

Terpenoids (specifically, ginkgolides and bilobalides): They help with dry nose by acting as mast cell stabilizers, which can prevent the release of histamine and other chemical mediators that contribute to nasal congestion and dryness.

Phenolic acids (specifically, quercetin and isorhapontigenin): They help with dry nose by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nasal mucosa and promote healthy nasal function.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most used part of Ginkgo biloba due to their high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids, which help to alleviate dry nose symptoms.
Barks: The barks are also commonly used, as they contain ginkgolides and bilobalide, compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties and help to relieve dry nose.
Leaves: The leaves are again used due to their ability to improve blood circulation, which can help to reduce nasal dryness by increasing oxygenation of the nasal tissues.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 pound of dried ginkgo biloba leaves and 2 cups of glycerin for encapsulation.
Mix 1000 mg of ginkgo biloba leaves with 1 tablespoon of glycerin for 30 minutes.
Combine the glycerin mixture with 2 cups of distilled water and heat it to 100 degrees fahrenheit for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids while retaining 100 ml of liquid.
Fill 1000 mg capsules with the liquid extract and seal them immediately to preserve potency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale capsules helps with dry nose because they contain natural anti-inflammatory properties found in ginger. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation and promote the production of mucus in the nasal passages. This increase in mucus helps to keep the nasal passages moist, relieving dryness and discomfort. Additionally, ginger's natural expectorant properties help to clear out mucus and debris, further reducing the symptoms of a dry nose.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds help alleviate dry nose symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting the production of mucus to keep the nasal passages moist.
Zingerone: As a natural expectorant, zingerone helps loosen and remove excess mucus, making it easier to breathe and reducing dryness in the nasal passages.

Volatile oils (mainly gingerols and shogaols): These oils possess decongestant properties, helping to reduce nasal congestion and promote easier breathing, which in turn helps alleviate dry nose symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes: These underground stems store nutrients and are rich in active compounds that help to thin mucus and relieve congestion.
Leaves: Leaves: The leaves contain volatile oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm a dry nose.
Roots: Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to protect the nasal passages and relieve dryness.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality zingiber officinale roots weighing about 500 grams and store them in an airtight container.
Dry the zingiber officinale roots in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind the dried zingiber officinale roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder at 500 rpm.
Mix the powder with 10 grams of gelatin and 100 milliliters of water to create a uniform blend.
Encapsulate the blend into size zero capsules using a gelatin capsule machine and store them in the fridge.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ephedra sinica capsules helps with dry nose because they contain the herb Ephedra sinica, which is traditionally used to relieve nasal congestion and sinus pressure. The active compounds, including ephedrine and ephedrine alkaloids, have natural decongestant properties that help to thin mucus and promote drainage, making it easier to breathe through the nose. Additionally, Ephedra sinica capsules may also have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the nasal passages, providing relief from dry, stuffy noses.
remedy_constituents: Ephedrine: Acts as a nasal decongestant by reducing nasal congestion and promoting mucus secretion, which helps to relieve a dry nose.
Norephedrine: Exhibits a similar effect to ephedrine in reducing nasal congestion and promoting mucus production, which can help alleviate dryness in the nasal passages.

Ephedrine Alkaloids (EAAs): Include compounds such as ephedrine, norephedrine, and pseudoephedrine that work together to relieve nasal congestion, promote mucus secretion, and help alleviate dryness in the nasal passages.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain high amounts of ephedrine, a key ingredient in relieving nasal congestion and dry nose symptoms.
Seeds: They are used to create extracts that help loosen mucus and provide relief from dryness and congestion in the nose.
Stems: They are often used in combination with other parts to create a balanced formula that helps to combat dry nose and nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ephedra sinica roots and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated to ensure quality.
Clean the harvested ephedra sinica material with a soft brush to remove dirt and debris.
Grind 20 grams of the cleaned ephedra sinica material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the ephedra sinica powder with 100 grams of gelatin powder to create a uniform mixture.
Fill empty capsules with 500 milligrams of the ephedra sinica and gelatin mixture and seal them.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus serpyllum capsules helps with dry nose because they contain the essential oils of Thymus serpyllum, also known as wild thyme, which have natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The capsules help to reduce nasal congestion and promote a healthy mucous membrane, allowing for easier breathing and relief from dry, irritated nasal passages. Additionally, the antioxidants present in Thymus serpyllum may help to soothe and protect the nasal tissue, promoting overall nasal health and reducing symptoms of dry nose.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Acts as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, thereby reducing dryness and congestion.
Carvacrol: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and irritation in the nasal passages and sinuses, which can contribute to dry nose symptoms.
Rosmarinic acid: Possesses antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to protect the nasal mucosa from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can exacerbate dry nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used to make capsules for dry nose due to their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Used to make capsules for dry nose due to their ability to soothe and calm the nasal passages.
Stems: Used to make capsules for dry nose due to their antimicrobial properties that help combat infections and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Dry 100g of fresh thymus serpyllum flowers in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 24 hours.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 10g of the powder with 90g of vegetable cellulose to create a uniform blend.
Fill 200 empty capsules with 0.5g of the herbal powder mixture using a capsule filler.
Label and store the herbal capsules in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis capsules helps with dry nose because they contain the essential oils of rosemary, which have natural decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The compounds in rosemary help to relax and reduce nasal congestion, allowing for easier breathing and moisturizing the nasal passages. Additionally, rosemary's antioxidant properties help to protect the nasal lining from damage caused by dry air, promoting a healthy and hydrated nasal environment. Regular use of rosemary capsules can provide long-lasting relief from dry nose symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Carnosic acid: Helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nasal passages, thereby alleviating dryness and discomfort.
Caryophyllene oxide: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm the nasal tissues, reducing dryness and irritation.
Bornyl acetate: Acts as a natural expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the nasal passages, thereby relieving dryness and congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make capsules for dry nose because they are a rich source of rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties.
Flowers: They are used to make capsules for dry nose because they contain essential oils that help to loosen and clear mucus, providing relief from congestion.
Stems: They are used to make capsules for dry nose because they contain rosmarinic acid and other bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of dry nose.
remedy_recipe: Dry 100g of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves at 50°c for 2 hours to reduce moisture content.
Grind 50g of dried rosmarinus officinalis leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 10g of powder with 50g of tapioca starch to create a uniform blend.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 0.5g of the herbal mixture using a capsule filler.
Label and store the capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris capsules helps with dry nose because of its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in thyme, such as thymol and carvacrol, work to break down mucus and reduce swelling in the nasal passages. This helps to relieve congestion and promote healthy drainage, making it easier to breathe and reducing the feeling of dryness in the nose. Additionally, thyme's antimicrobial properties help to combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the dryness.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol has antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the nasal passages, providing relief from dry nose symptoms.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to thin mucus and promote its expulsion, thereby reducing congestion and dryness in the nasal passages.
Bornyl acetate: Bornyl acetate has expectorant and mucolytic properties, which help to break down and clear mucus from the nasal passages, reducing congestion and dryness associated with a dry nose.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are commonly used for their decongestant properties, helping to relieve dry nose and congestion.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain thymol, a natural decongestant that helps to reduce nasal congestion and relieve dry nose symptoms.
Stems: Thyme stems are also used for their decongestant properties, containing compounds that help to relieve dry nose and promote nasal airflow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated for best quality.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 6 hours.
Grind 500 grams of dried thymus vulgaris into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix 10 grams of the powder with 200 grams of gelatin capsules and fill the capsules.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules helps with dry nose because they contain licorice root extract, which is rich in glycyrrhizic acid. This compound has potent anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and moisturize the nasal passages, reducing dryness and irritation. The anti-inflammatory properties of licorice root also help to reduce swelling and congestion, allowing for easier breathing and improved nasal function. Regular use of Glycyrrhiza glabra capsules may help to alleviate dry nose symptoms and promote a healthier respiratory system.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps to reduce inflammation and promote mucous production, thereby relieving dry nose symptoms.
Liquiritin: It acts as a natural expectorant, helping to thin and clear mucus, and also has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the nasal passages.
Isoflavans: They have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help reduce nasal congestion and promote a healthy mucous balance, alleviating dry nose symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which helps in soothing and moisturizing the nasal passages.
Leaves: They are used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing nasal congestion and promoting nasal health.
Barks: They are used for their astringent properties, which help in drying up excessive mucus and reducing nasal congestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra in late summer or early fall when they are mature and dry.
Clean the roots thoroughly by washing them in distilled water to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the cleaned roots at room temperature or using a food dehydrator at 50 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle at a ratio of 1 part root to 1 part capsule filler.
Fill empty vegetarian capsules with the powder at a dosage of 200-300 milligrams per capsule and seal them immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa capsules helps with dry nose because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the nasal passages. The bioactive compound curcumin, found in these capsules, has natural decongestant and moisturizing effects that help to hydrate and protect the mucous membranes in the nose. By reducing inflammation and promoting moisture, Curcuma longa capsules alleviate dryness and discomfort associated with dry nose, promoting a healthy and balanced nasal environment. Regular use can lead to improved respiratory comfort and overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Curcuminoids: Help with dry nose by reducing inflammation and relieving congestion, which in turn helps to alleviate dryness and discomfort.
Tumerones: Help with dry nose by reducing mucus viscosity and promoting its clearance, thus relieving dryness and congestion associated with dry nose.
Demethoxycurcumin: Help with dry nose by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, which in turn helps to alleviate dryness and inflammation associated with dry nose.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are commonly used as they contain high concentrations of curcumin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate dry nose symptoms.
Leaves: Leaves are often used due to their high content of essential oils, which can provide relief from dryness and irritation in the nasal passages.
Stems: Stems are utilized for their ability to contain bioactive compounds that can help soothe and moisturize the nasal area, thereby alleviating dry nose symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest curcuma longa roots in the early morning when the dew is still present on the plants.
Clean the roots thoroughly under running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm dry place for 7-10 days to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 400mg of the curcuma longa powder and seal immediately.
